purpose: Mechanics of the dispatch phase: how the master spawns workers via the Task tool, how dispatch prompts are constructed (TEMPLATE_VERSION, paths, FILES, CRITICAL, UPSTREAM RESULTS), and how parallel dispatch is achieved by issuing multiple Task tool calls in a single message. Covers eager dispatch (dispatch as soon as deps are satisfied) vs wave-gated dispatch.
gotchas: Multiple Task tool calls must go in a SINGLE message to actually run in parallel; sequential messages serialize
Each dispatch prompt is fully self-contained -- workers cannot call back to the master
patterns: Self-contained dispatch prompts with full context inlined
Eager dispatch where dependency satisfaction immediately unblocks downstream tasks
conventions: Prompts always include both {tracker_root} and {project_root} explicitly
